bucket_summary.diagnosis: Single failed-login events are still being treated as repeated guessing.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add anti-gravity normal examples with failed_attempts=1, count=1, isolated 4625, single 401/403 responses, and no burst/correlation evidence.
bucket_summary.title: Normal -> brute force

bucket_summary.diagnosis: Reconnaissance signals are still pulled into brute force when wording is generic.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add port/recon positives with nmap fingerprint, service enumeration, horizontal scan, unique_hosts/unique_ports thresholds, and blocked/attempt wording.
bucket_summary.title: Port/recon -> brute force

bucket_summary.diagnosis: Login/API/status context still overrules clear SQL injection payloads.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add SQLi positives in login, API, search, and reset-password contexts with 401/403/400/500 status codes, keeping quote tautologies, UNION, SLEEP/WAITFOR, stacked queries, and comment markers prominent.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi -> brute force

bucket_summary.diagnosis: Quote-heavy SQLi evidence can still break JSON generation despite structured outputs.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add quote-heavy SQLi examples and keep evidence as short exact substrings that require JSON escaping, then probe structured-output enforcement on the same shape.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi -> invalid

bucket_summary.diagnosis: Schema-discovery or SQL vocabulary is still underweighted in request-parameter context.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add information_schema and encoded SQLi positives paired with benign documentation/search hard negatives that contain SQL words without payload boundaries.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi -> normal

bucket_summary.diagnosis: SQL quote/comment syntax is still confused with file/path injection syntax.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add contrast pairs where SQL comment markers such as admin'-- are adjacent to traversal paths such as ../../etc/passwd and encoded ../ sequences.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi -> traversal

bucket_summary.diagnosis: Status/blocking cues can still trigger the over-strong brute-force label.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add traversal positives with 401/403/blocked wording and no authentication burst evidence.
bucket_summary.title: Traversal -> brute force

bucket_summary.diagnosis: Sensitive path traversal tokens are underweighted when the log looks routine.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add traversal positives with ../, encoded traversal, /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, win.ini, secrets.yml, %00, and php://filter while preserving benign relative-path hard negatives.
bucket_summary.title: Traversal -> normal

bucket_summary.diagnosis: Status, user-agent, curl, and generic probing cues distract from traversal paths.
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add traversal positives with distracting curl/scanner-like user agents and blocked/404 status codes, keeping the sensitive path token prominent.
bucket_summary.title: Traversal -> port/recon

dataset_implications.details: Use login/API/search/reset contexts with 401/403/400/500, quote tautologies, UNION SELECT, SLEEP/WAITFOR, stacked queries, comment markers, encoded payloads, and quote-heavy evidence.
Use ../, encoded %2e%2e, /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, win.ini, secrets.yml, %00, php://filter, and distracting status/user-agent cues.
Use single auth failures, isolated 4625, benign SQL docs/search terms, benign relative paths, allowlisted inventory, and low-scope monitoring.
Use nmap fingerprint, service enumeration, horizontal scan, unique_hosts/unique_ports, and blocked/attempt wording that should not become brute force.
v3.5 should reduce brute-force gravity rather than further strengthening the label.

next_step: Create the v3.5 boundary repair supplement from these buckets, then train and probe v3.5 on hard-contrast before any mini semantic or fixed test comparison.
summary: v3.5 slices the v3.4 temperature=0 hard-contrast failures so the next repair can target SQLi, traversal, invalid JSON, and brute-force gravity without using the fixed test split.
